seo.title: Semaglutide Tracker: Dose, Refill and Side-Effect Log
seo.description: Join the Semaglutide Tracker waitlist for a private, evidence-first workflow covering dose history, reminders, refill timing, and side-effect notes.

trust.disclaimer: This landing page describes a planned tracking workflow, not medical advice. Actual use, dose changes, and missed-dose decisions should still follow official instructions and clinician guidance.

title: Semaglutide Tracker
summary: A private semaglutide workflow for people who want one place to log dose history, refill timing, symptom notes, and treatment milestones without forcing a social or marketplace experience.
supportedFeatures: Printable treatment summary for clinician visits
privacySummary: The tracking roadmap is local-first and privacy-first by default. PeptideScholar does not position the tracker as a diagnostic or prescribing tool.
availabilitySummary: The tracker is not publicly launched yet. Join the waitlist to get rollout updates and early access when the web-based version is ready.

faqs.question: What problem is the Semaglutide Tracker meant to solve?
faqs.answer: It is meant to reduce the mental overhead around dose history, refill timing, side-effect notes, and treatment continuity for people following semaglutide workflows.

faqs.question: Is the tracker a replacement for clinical advice?
faqs.answer: No. It is designed as a private adherence and note-taking layer that sits around official instructions and clinician follow-up, not a replacement for either.

faqs.question: Why build a dedicated semaglutide landing page before the full app launches?
faqs.answer: Because semaglutide has strong intent across treatment, legal, cost, and reminder-related queries, and this page lets PeptideScholar validate demand before overbuilding the app.

cta.notes: Start with the waitlist if you want early access; use the cost page if you still need to choose a treatment path.
